title: रुक्मिणी मङ्गलाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री रुक्मिणी मङ्गलाष्टकम् ॥

पार्वतीपतये देव्यै सरस्वत्यै नमो नमः।
वेदवेदाङ्गरूपाय श्रीकृष्णाय नमो नमः॥

रत्नसिंहासनासीनां श्रीकृष्णवरपूजिताम्।
जगन्मातां सदा वन्दे रुक्मिणीं मङ्गलप्रदाम्॥ १॥

भीष्मकस्य कुलोद्भूतां विदर्भनगरीश्वरीम्।
कृष्णकान्तां महाभागां रुक्मिणीं मङ्गलं दिशतु॥ २॥

स्वयंवरे हरेर्भार्यां ययाचे शम्बरो बली।
निजतेजोबलेनैव कृष्णो निन्ये रणाङ्गणे।
तस्यै रुक्मिणी देव्यै मङ्गलं शुभमङ्गलम्॥ ३॥

द्वारकापुरनिवासिनीं शुभां
शंखचक्रधरपत्नी सुन्दरीम्।
वैजयन्तीमालया विराजितां
रुक्मिणीं प्रणमामि मङ्गलप्रदाम्॥ ४॥

प्रद्युम्नजननीं शुद्धां चारुदेष्णप्रसूतिकाम्।
साम्बमातामहावीर्यां रुक्मिणीं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

पद्महस्तां पद्मनेत्रां पद्मवर्णां सुशोभनाम्।
पद्मालयां पद्मिनीं च रुक्मिणीं मङ्गलं श्रयेत्॥ ६॥

क्षीरसागरसम्भूतां विष्णुवक्षःस्थलस्थिताम्।
सर्वलोकैकवन्द्यां तां रुक्मिणीं मङ्गलं भजे॥ ७॥

नारायणप्रियां लक्ष्मीं विश्वमातरमीश्वरीम्।
सर्वसौभाग्यदात्रीं तां रुक्मिणीं मङ्गलं स्तुमः॥ ८॥

रुक्मिणीमङ्गलाष्टकं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
सौभाग्यं लभते नित्यं गृहे लक्ष्मीः सदा वसेत्॥

॥ इति श्री रुक्मिणी मङ्गलाष्टकम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Rukmini Mangalashtakam ||

Parvati-pataye devyai Sarasvatyai namo namah.
Veda-Vedanga-rupaya Shri Krishnaya namo namah.

Ratna-simhasana-asinam Shri Krishna-vara-pujitam.
Jagan-matam sada vande Rukminim mangala-pradam. || 1 ||

Bhishmakasya kulod-bhutam Vidarbha-nagari-ishvarim.
Krishna-kantam Maha-bhagam Rukminim mangalam dishatu. || 2 ||

Svayamvare Harer-bharyam yayache Shambaro bali.
Nija-tejo-balenaiva Krishno ninye rananganane.
Tasyai Rukmini devyai mangalam shubha-mangalam. || 3 ||

Dvaraka-pura-nivasi nim shubham
Shankha-chakra-dhara-patni sundarim.
Vaijayanti-malaya virajitam
Rukminim pranamami mangala-pradam. || 4 ||

Pradyumna-jananim shudham Charudesna-prasutikam.
Samba-matam-aha-viryam Rukminim pranamamyaham. || 5 ||

Padma-hastam padma-netram padma-varnam sushobhanam.
Padma-alayam Padminim cha Rukminim mangalam shrayet. || 6 ||

Kshira-sagara-sambhutam Vishnu-vakshah-sthala-sthitam.
Sarva-lokaika-vandyam tam Rukminim mangalam bhaje. || 7 ||

Narayana-priyam Lakshmim Vishva-mataram-ishvarim.
Sarva-saubhagya-datrim tam Rukminim mangalam stumah. || 8 ||

Rukmini-mangala-ashtakam yah pathed-bhakti-samyutah.
Saubhagyam labhate nityam grihe Lakshmih sada vaset.

|| Iti Shri Rukmini Mangalashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
रत्नसिंहासन पर विराजमान, श्रीकृष्ण द्वारा पूजित, जगत की माता, मंगलप्रदा रुक्मिणी को मैं सदा वन्दन करता हूँ॥ १॥

भीष्मक के कुल में उत्पन्न, विदर्भ नगरी की ईश्वरी, कृष्ण की प्रिय, महाभाग्यशालिनी रुक्मिणी मंगल दें॥ २॥

स्वयंवर में बलवान शम्बर ने हरि की पत्नी को चाहा, किन्तु कृष्ण ने अपने तेज और बल से रणभूमि में उन्हें जीतकर ले गए। उन रुक्मिणी देवी को शुभ मंगल हो॥ ३॥

द्वारकापुर निवासिनी, शुभ, शंखचक्रधारी की पत्नी, सुन्दरी, वैजयन्ती माला से विराजित, मंगलप्रदा रुक्मिणी को प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ४॥

प्रद्युम्न की जननी, शुद्ध, चारुदेष्ण को जन्म देने वाली, साम्ब की माता, महावीर्यशालिनी रुक्मिणी को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ५॥

कमल हाथ वाली, कमल नेत्री, कमल वर्ण, सुशोभन, कमलालया पद्मिनी रुक्मिणी मंगलकारी हों॥ ६॥

क्षीरसागर से प्रकट, विष्णु के वक्षस्थल पर निवास करने वाली, सर्वलोक वन्दनीया रुक्मिणी का मैं मंगल भजन करता हूँ॥ ७॥

नारायण की प्रिय, लक्ष्मी, विश्वमाता, ईश्वरी, सर्व सौभाग्य प्रदान करने वाली रुक्मिणी का हम मंगल स्तवन करते हैं। इसे पढ़ने वाले को नित्य सौभाग्य मिलता है और घर में सदा लक्ष्मी निवास करती हैं॥ ८॥
benefits: Invokes the blessing of marital bliss and harmony
Invokes the grace of goddess lakshmi (rukmini)
Traditionally recited to invoke perpetual prosperity and good fortune in the household
Ideal for recitation during marriages and auspicious occasions
Traditionally recited for removal of domestic discord and brings peace
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires for happy family life
description: Dedicated to Lord Krishna, the Rukmini Mangalashtakam (रुक्मिणी मङ्गलाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Traditional Sanskrit Stotra, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to ensure perpetual prosperity and good fortune in the household and grant the grace of Goddess Lakshmi (Rukmini). It is also said to bestow marital bliss and harmony. This prayer is traditionally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami, Diwali, and Vivah Panchami to invoke divine grace. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Rukmini Mangalashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Rukmini Mangalashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rukmini Mangalashtakam (रुक्मिणी मङ्गलाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse mangala-stotra in praise of Devi Rukmini — the principal queen (pattamahishi) of Lord Krishna in his Dvaraka leela and an avatar of Goddess Mahalakshmi. Preserved in the traditional Sanskrit stotra corpus, the hymn invokes auspiciousness (mangalam) upon Rukmini through her many epithets: Bhishmaka-kulodbhuta (born in King Bhishmaka's lineage), Vidarbha-nagari-ishvari (sovereign of Vidarbha), Krishna-kanta (beloved of Krishna), Pradyumna-janani (mother of Pradyumna, Charudeshna and Samba), Padmahasta, Kshirasagara-sambhuta (Lakshmi born of the milk-ocean), Vishnu-vakshasthala-sthita and Narayana-priya. Each verse celebrates a distinct dimension of Rukmini as Krishna-pattamahishi and as Lakshmi-avatara. (Source: Traditional Sanskrit stotra; Bhagavata Rukmini-vivaha tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Rukmini Mangalashtakam recited at weddings (Vivah Panchami)?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rukmini Mangalashtakam is the central wedding-mangalam stotra in Krishna devotion because it celebrates the most famous svayamvara-vivaha in Krishna's leela — Krishna's heroic abduction of Rukmini from Vidarbha and their subsequent marriage at Dvaraka. Verse 3 specifically commemorates this episode: Svayamvare Harer-bharyam yayache Shambaro bali, Nija-tejo-balenaiva Krishno ninye rananganane — 'when the powerful Shambara desired Hari's bride at the svayamvara, Krishna by his own brilliance and strength carried her away from the battlefield.' The hymn's invocation of mangalam upon this divine couple is therefore deeply auspicious for any new marriage, and is recited on Vivah Panchami (Margashirsha Shukla Panchami — celebrated as the wedding anniversary in some Vaishnava traditions) and at human weddings to invoke the Krishna-Rukmini blessing. (Source: Bhagavata Purana Rukmini-haranam; Vivah Panchami tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Rukmini identified as Mahalakshmi in this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 7-8 explicitly identify Rukmini as the avatar of Mahalakshmi: Kshira-sagara-sambhutam Vishnu-vakshah-sthala-sthitam — 'born of the milk-ocean, residing on Vishnu's chest' — both classical Lakshmi epithets from the Samudra-manthana legend; and Narayana-priyam Lakshmim Vishva-mataram-ishvarim — 'beloved of Narayana, Lakshmi, Mother of the universe, Sovereign Goddess.' The Bhagavata Purana, the Padma Purana and the Vaishnava Pancharatra all teach that whenever Vishnu incarnates, Lakshmi accompanies him: Sita with Rama, Padmavati with Venkateshwara, and Rukmini with Krishna. Rukmini-Krishna are therefore the Dvaraka manifestation of the eternal Lakshmi-Narayana couple, and the mangalam invoked in this stotra is simultaneously a Lakshmi-mangalam. (Source: Bhagavata Purana; Pancharatra Lakshmi-Narayana tattva.)

seoFaqs.question: When and on what occasions should the Rukmini Mangalashtakam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rukmini Mangalashtakam is most powerful on Friday (Shukravar — Lakshmi's day) and Wednesday (Budhvar — Krishna's day), and is especially auspicious on Vivah Panchami (Margashirsha Shukla Panchami), Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami — when Krishna was born), Rukmini Ashtami (Margashirsha Krishna Ashtami in some traditions — Rukmini's special day), Diwali (when Lakshmi is worshipped — and Rukmini as Lakshmi-avatara is invoked), Akshaya Tritiya, the four months of Chaturmasya, and during all Hindu wedding ceremonies as part of the mangalashtakam recitation that traditionally accompanies the saptapadi vows. Householders chant it for marital harmony, for the birth of virtuous children (like Pradyumna), and for the perpetual residence of Lakshmi in the home. (Source: Vaishnava mangala-paath tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Rukmini Mangalashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares: Rukmini-mangala-ashtakam yah pathed-bhakti-samyutah, Saubhagyam labhate nityam grihe Lakshmih sada vaset — 'one who recites this Rukmini Mangalashtakam with bhakti permanently obtains saubhagya (marital auspiciousness) and Lakshmi forever resides in their home.' Traditional benefits include: marital harmony and bliss between husband and wife (modelled on the ideal Krishna-Rukmini relationship); perpetual residence of Lakshmi in the household; bestowal of virtuous, capable children (like Pradyumna, Charudeshna and Samba); removal of domestic discord and quarrels; protection from family enemies (as Krishna protected Rukmini from Shishupala and Shambara); fulfilment of saubhagya for unmarried women seeking a Krishna-like husband; and the lasting blessings of both Krishna-bhakti and Lakshmi-prasada. (Source: Rukmini Mangalashtakam phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Rukmini Mangalashtakam differ from the Krishna Mangalashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Krishna Mangalashtakam invokes mangalam upon Lord Krishna himself, traversing the eight stages of his life from Vasudeva-suta birth to Partha-sarathi at Kurukshetra — its scope is Krishna's entire avatara-leela. The Rukmini Mangalashtakam, by contrast, invokes mangalam upon Rukmini Devi as Krishna's principal queen, focusing on her royal birth in Vidarbha, the heroic Krishna-led svayamvara at Dvaraka, her motherhood of Pradyumna, her identity as Lakshmi-avatara, and her role as the supreme grihalakshmi (queen of the household). The two stotras are commonly recited together at weddings and during Janmashtami / Vivah Panchami celebrations to invoke the joint mangalam of the divine couple — the Krishna-Rukmini Yugala. (Source: Vaishnava mangalashtakam tradition.)
